name: JR Shinjuku Station B1F Coin Lockers (East/Central East Gate)
description: Nearest large locker bank to Kabukicho; approximately 3,600 lockers station-wide; large lockers are frequently full by 10 AM.

name: JR Shinjuku Station East Exit Underground Passage Lockers
description: 8 lockers across 4 sub-banks near the East Exit ticket gates.

name: Lumine Est Shinjuku 1F Coin Lockers
description: Locker banks at the East Exit taxi-stand loop and along the Lumine Est department-store wall, plus inside near the ticket gates.

name: JR Shinjuku Station West Exit Underground Plaza Lockers
description: Largest locker bank at Shinjuku Station: 131 lockers, 17 large-size.

name: JR Shinjuku Station South Exit Concourse Lockers
description: Multiple banks parallel to and inside the South Exit ticket gates, plus basement banks via stairs/escalators.

name: Seibu-Shinjuku Station North Exit Lockers
description: Cashless-only (IC card or SPACER app) lockers near the North Exit ticket machines; S/M/L/XL at ¥100/200/300/400 per 4hr, open 7:00-23:59, 96hr storage limit.

name: Seibu-Shinjuku Station Underground Passage / Subnade Lockers
description: Locker bank in the underground passage connecting Seibu-Shinjuku Station to the Shinjuku Subnade mall.

name: Tokyu Kabukicho Tower B1F Coin Lockers
description: 24h large locker bank (300+ units) at B1F, ~1 min from the Kabukicho Ichibangai arch entrance. Not listed on the tower's own FAQ, which advertises only a 1F staffed baggage desk instead — independently reported by multiple visitor guides.
gray_zone_note: Not listed on Tokyu Kabukicho Tower's own official FAQ, which advertises only a 1F staffed baggage desk -- reported independently by multiple visitor guides but not confirmed by the venue itself. Verify on-site before relying on it.

name: Tokyu Kabukicho Tower 4F Cinema-Floor Lockers
description: Small electronic-payment-only lockers by the elevators on the cinema floor; ¥200 small / ¥300 medium, 180-minute limit, intended for moviegoers.

name: Tokyu Kabukicho Tower 10F (109 Cinemas Premium) Lockers
description: Cashless-only lockers (IC/tap/QR) beside the elevators on the 10F 109 Cinemas Premium Shinjuku floor.

name: Zepp Shinjuku Lockers (B1F/B4F)
description: 840 lockers total (690 at B1F, 150 at B4F) inside the Tokyu Kabukicho Tower basement venue; cash-only ¥400, change machines on site. Usable only after entering past the ticket gate, not for pre-entry storage.

name: Humax Pavilion Shinjuku Kabukicho Coin Lockers
description: 24h cash-payment lockers in the leisure building ~1 min from Tokyu Kabukicho Tower; ¥300 small / ¥400 medium.

name: Hotel WAKO Shinjuku Parking-Side Coin Locker
description: Street-level coin locker beside the Hotel WAKO parking entrance, ~3 min from Seibu-Shinjuku Station North Exit toward the hotel-gai.

name: Adhoc Shinjuku Building Coin Lockers
description: Two locker clusters (2F elevator front and a stairwell-side bank), ~1 min from Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line Shinjuku-sanchome exits B8/B9, on Kabukicho's southern edge.

name: Manboo Manboo Kabukicho Net-Room Entrance Locker
description: Coin locker near the entrance of the Manboo Manboo internet café/net-room, ~5 min from Seibu-Shinjuku/Higashi-Shinjuku Station.

name: Godzilla Road / Karaoke-kan Kabukicho Main Store Entrance Locker
description: Coin locker at the entrance of Karaoke-kan's Kabukicho Main Store on Godzilla Road, the district's central street; operated by Fuji Service Co.

name: ecbo cloak — H.F. Kabukicho Building (1-2-2)
description: App-reserved, coin-locker-priced staffed luggage storage inside Kabukicho itself (1-2-2). Suitcase-friendly with no rigid size cap, unlike physical lockers — best fallback when a bag is too large for any coin locker.
